title: Gnaphalium retortum
intro: Gnaphalium retortum, also known as Buttonweed or Button Gnaphalium, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, commonly found in the temperate regions of North America and Eurasia. The plant is valued for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gnaphalium retortum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and triterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gnaphalium retortum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders.
taxonomy: Gnaphalium retortum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a member of the tribe Gnaphalieae, which includes many species of daisy-like plants. Gnaphalium retortum is further classified as a genus within the tribe, being one of the many species within this group. The species is native to Australia, where it can be found growing in a variety of habitats. Its classification is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf and flower morphology.
